value.name: Compass Card
value.notes: Reloadable tap card for Metro Vancouver transit. Tap in and out on SkyTrain, SeaBus, and West Coast Express; tap on when boarding buses. Buy at station vending machines, London Drugs, or compasscard.ca. A YVR AddFare (CA$6.50 as of July 1, 2026) applies when exiting the airport SkyTrain station.

tipping.value: 15–20% on the pre-tax total at sit-down restaurants; payment terminals usually prompt 15/18/20%. Not expected at counter/quick service. Roughly 15% for taxis, and a couple of dollars per bag or per night for porters and housekeeping.

value.name: Transit
value.why: Real-time SkyTrain, bus, and SeaBus departures plus trip planning across Metro Vancouver.

value.name: TransLink
value.why: Official Metro Vancouver transit authority — service alerts, schedules, and Compass Card info.

entryRules.value: Visa-exempt foreign nationals need an eTA (CA$7, linked to the passport, valid up to 5 years) to fly into Canada; it is not required when arriving by car, bus, train, or boat. US citizens are exempt. Other travellers may need a visitor visa.

cashNorms.value: Cards and contactless (tap) are accepted almost everywhere and are the norm; carry a little cash for tips, small vendors, and farmers' markets. Listed prices are pre-tax — GST and PST are added at checkout.

value.notes: Jun–Aug: warm, dry, and sunny with highs around 20–23°C — the driest season and the best window for the outdoors.
Dec–Feb: mild but wet, roughly 0–10°C, with rain far more common than snow downtown. Pack a waterproof layer.
